content: The 2024-2025 SOTA trend of progressive, context-gated active memory compression in active learning agents can be repurposed to resolve a longstanding flaw of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (ICM/RND): the reward collapse from over-exploration of trivial, high-novelty noisy distractors. By ranking encountered states for active memory retention based on both intrinsic curiosity signal and downstream task relevance, agents avoid wasting memory and exploration reward on uninformative random noise while retaining high-value surprising states that improve generalization.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
